description: The Manson Family was a commune, gang, and cult led by Charles Manson, active in California from the late 1960s to early 1970s. The group consisted of approximately 100 followers who lived an unconventional lifestyle, using psychoactive drugs and radicalized by Manson's teachings. They committed at least nine murders, including the Tate–LaBianca murders, and other crimes. The group dissolved after Manson and other members were arrested.

country_links.evidence_quote: Former Family member Barbara Hoyt... agreed to accompany the latter to Hawaii. There, Moorehouse allegedly gave her a hamburger spiked with several doses of LSD.
country_links.context: A Family member took a witness to Hawaii and drugged her there.

country_links.evidence_quote: Watson returned to McKinney, Texas after the Tate–LaBianca murders. He was arrested in Texas on November 30, 1969...
country_links.context: Family member Tex Watson returned to Texas after the murders and was arrested there.

country_links.evidence_quote: In 1969, Manson Family members Atkins, Charles Denton 'Tex' Watson, and Patricia Krenwinkel entered the home of actress Sharon Tate and murdered her and four others.
country_links.context: The Manson Family operated in California, committed murders in Los Angeles, and had members active in Sacramento and other states.

country_links.evidence_quote: Fromme... escaped briefly from Federal Prison Camp, Alderson in West Virginia.
country_links.context: A Family member escaped from a prison camp in West Virginia.
